title: test_models: add a test that fuzzes the tx messages
author: sshane
body:
Currently we compare the car states from panda and openpilot to ensure that both parse CAN messages with the same 1) validity checks and 2) correctness/no bugs. That let us fix quite a few mismatches in behavior between the two: https://github.com/commaai/openpilot/pull/30443

Now we want to fuzz the tx messages as well, to discover bugs related to controlsAllowed in panda, and general tx safety. Here is one such mismatch that we should aim to catch: https://github.com/commaai/panda/pull/1948

See the first PR linked above for rx to know where to start. Bounty is for a merged PR that adds fuzz testing for sent openpilot messages that
1) detects mismatches in tx (sent messages) logic between openpilot and panda,
1) extensible (generic, and easy to expand with more state),
2) as performant as our current fuzzy testing, and
3) covers as many cases as possible (catches a few bugs along the way, if there are any).

hunter_intelligence.technical_hint: Review the existing fuzzing implementation for rx messages in the linked PR and adapt it to target tx message logic, ensuring compatibility with both openpilot and panda's safety checks.

title: Match stock Hyundai button logic
author: sshane
body:
Currently we diverge from the original behavior when openpilot (alpha) longitudinal is used, which has confused some users in the past having them ask on Discord how to engage.

We should match the stock longitudinal button logic in panda and openpilot, here's what we don't match:
- [ ] CAN cars with set, resume, and main on button
  - [ ] Match main on button, which is a grate on whether cruise is "available". For example, pressing it has no effect on availability, nor resetting cruise speed, etc. 
  - [ ] Resume should not be allowed if no cruise speed set (immediately after toggling main/availability)
- [ ] CAN and CAN FD cars with set, [pause/resume](https://github.com/commaai/openpilot/assets/25857203/5402ad78-2599-4232-b673-aed62a8fb270), and main on button that toggles cruise:
  - [ ] Above criteria for main button, in addition to enabling cruise on rising edge the first time.
  - [ ] Pause/resume button should toggle cruise on and off if cruise is available.
- [ ] Not all HKG cars have this main and pause/resume button functionality and this button shows up as the normal resume button to openpilot, so we ideally need a way to differentiate these buttons/cars.

This needs to be done for both Hyundai's CAN and CAN FD platforms.

hunter_intelligence.technical_hint: Review the Hyundai-specific logic in `selfdrive/car/hyundai` and `panda/board/safety/safety_hyundai.h` to align button behavior with stock functionality, focusing on differentiating CAN and CAN FD signals for main and pause/resume buttons.

body:
### Describe the bug

There's currently an issue with F150 Lightnings on the Ford Q4 Harness/CAN-FD platform. This is specific to the Lightning as the ICE variants do not have this issue nor do the Mustang Mach Es.

1. Install Ford Q4 Harness into F150 Lightning.
2. Turn on Vehicle and notice no errors
3. Install Comma Power and plug into harness
4. Turn on vehicle and notice no errors
5. Plug in Comma provided USB-C cable to harness (leaving unplugged from Comma 3/3X)
6. Notice the number of errors received (Traction Control, Park Assist, One Pedal Drive)
7. Plug in Comma 3X (with a working release of OpenPilot) and notice issue persist.
8. Allow Openpilot to fully boot and open the relay on the harness and observe the car faults disappearing

### Which car does this affect?

2022+ Ford F150 Lightning

### Provide a route where the issue occurs

N/A - Happens prior to openpilot

### openpilot version

0.9.5

### Additional info

I've spoken with Discord and they're pretty confused on why this is happening as well. Additionally, there's now 2 Lightnings that are observing this behavior -- mine and one other persons.

I've also emailed support and they were a bit confused as well.

hunter_intelligence.technical_hint: Investigate the Ford Q4 Harness initialization sequence and CAN-FD message handling for potential misconfigurations or conflicts when the USB-C cable is connected but OpenPilot is not actively running.

title: Speedup CI setup to <20s
author: adeebshihadeh
body:
The best case time of the `setup-with-retry` stage that runs in most of our CI jobs is ~1m4s. All it does is setup the openpilot environment, and most of that time is pulling an already built docker image. This puts a hard limit on how fast our jobs can finish; a job that finishes in 1m is 10x better than one that finishes in 2-3m.

Some possible strategies:
* make the docker image smaller
* move the docker cache somewhere faster
* ditch docker, install directly on the 20.04 runner, and cache the apt and pip packages

Requirements for the bounty:
* all `setup-with-retry` on the final PR commit must finish in less than <20s
* must run on the normal free-for-open-source GH actions runner

Sub-bounty of $100 for <40s if you can't get to <20s. $500 is for <20s. Bounties don't stack.

https://github.com/commaai/openpilot/blob/master/.github/workflows/setup-with-retry/action.yaml

hunter_intelligence.technical_hint: Review the issue details and related code.

title: [$500 Bounty] Switch from fastboot to EDL/QDL
author: adeebshihadeh
body:
[QDL](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Qualcomm_EDL_mode) should be a strict improvement
* unbrickable
* easier to get into than the [fastboot dance](https://youtu.be/gNnRmEyVSVQ?t=88)
* 2 -> 1 cables required
* save 3s off the boot time of the device waiting for fastboot USB enumeration

Seems like there's two options here:
1. flash the whole thing with firehose
2. load an EDL loader with fastboot and use the existing stuff to flash

Here's a good Python implementation: https://github.com/bkerler/edl.

* PR title should be something like "enable misra-c2012-2.2"

title: [$500 bounty] Ford: reliable fuzzy fingerprinting
author: sshane
body:
**Context:** You will improve the reliability of Ford fingerprinting by giving openpilot an understanding of the FW versions.

We have already implemented FW version parsing with specific brand understanding for [Hyundai](https://github.com/commaai/openpilot/blob/292f2ea2884f52b0e2729c2b493521546197a5b3/selfdrive/car/hyundai/values.py#L313-L380) and [Toyota](https://github.com/commaai/openpilot/blob/292f2ea2884f52b0e2729c2b493521546197a5b3/selfdrive/car/toyota/values.py#L255-L330). For Hyundai, we have the human-readable platform codes that the auto manufacturer uses, then we filter the fuzzy match by part date for a rough model year identifier. For Toyota, we parse out a region-specific platform code + a version code that describes new control APIs.

Check out recent Ford fingerprinting PRs, which ECUs are getting added more often?

**Goal:** Implement the same type of fuzzy fingerprinting for Ford, which seems to have patterns we can use to identify platforms. The solution should detect and fail to fingerprint a platform with breaking API changes, like a new vehicle generation with new control messages. Conversely, it should also fingerprint every unseen Ford Bronco with a very minimal amount of FW from one seen.

Certain Ford forums may have user-provided FW dumps to check your theories with, since openpilot has only largely seen one generation of each platform.

Required:
- [ ] works for all supported cars, plus the dashcam Q4 platform cars
- [ ] Parse the platform code with a way to split API/MY differences
- [ ] Choose certain or all ECUs to do fuzzy fingerprinting on
- [ ] Tests, if necessary
- [ ] Ship to release with same quality as the other custom fuzzy fingerprinting functions

title: [$200 bounty] New Ubuntu setup flow
author: adeebshihadeh
body:
See the current setup at `tools/ubuntu_setup.sh`. The goal of this bounty is to make the whole setup more interactive and describe what it's going to do. 

In the setup README, we'll offer two paths:
1. manually install/setup everything, for those who like to manage their own env and tooling
2. an interactive script that'll get you setup from scratch

The interactive script will go something like this:
* do you want to install these runtime apt dependencies?
* do you want to install apt development dependencies?
* do you want to install apt nice-to-haves (e.g. valgrind)?
* detect minimum python version
* start a venv

title: Replace React with something simple
author: adeebshihadeh
body:
Replace next/react/etc with a simpler stack. This will be a try-out for [comma connect's](https://github.com/commaai/connect) next framework.

We care about simplicity, brevity, and performance.  Some contenders:
* https://www.solidjs.com/
* https://svelte.dev/

Nice site with benchmarks of a bunch of frameworks: https://krausest.github.io/js-framework-benchmark/current.html

---

EDIT: Some more context on what we're looking for:

I just wrote [comma.ai/leaderboard](https://comma.ai/leaderboard). It loads instantly; you click refresh and don't even see it load. It has a defined JS-less experience and only uses JS where absolutely necessary. I simply started with `vi leaderboard.html` and got to work.

It was a joy to write, but there were a few pain points:
* preview was slow to refresh
* integrating it with webpack and the rest of the build system
* development vs production (minified version stripped out some spacing)

title: Car docs bot: diff on generated readme file
author: sshane
body:
We have a bot that uses the car docs Python data structures and prints a pretty diff to each PR, if it changes any car docs. That could be vehicle name, supported trim, minimum steering speed, parts, or detail sentence for the website.

Current bot output: https://github.com/commaai/openpilot/pull/31888#issuecomment-2000768679

Our current solution requires checkouts and pickling, which is fragile: [print_docs_diff.py](https://github.com/commaai/openpilot/blob/master/selfdrive/debug/print_docs_diff.py) and [selfdrive_tests.yaml#L256-L308](https://github.com/commaai/openpilot/blob/c514ee7212f7ef43c7451d6e638dd54ec0643dcc/.github/workflows/selfdrive_tests.yaml#L256-L308).

Solution
---

Just use the base and head commit CARS.md files in the PR, for example:

```
wget -O master_table.md https://raw.githubusercontent.com/commaai/openpilot/master/docs/CARS.md
diff master_table.md docs/CARS.md
```

See the generated CARS.md README file: https://github.com/commaai/openpilot/blob/master/docs/CARS.md

Diffing behavior:
- [ ] Should be agnostic to the columns and other changes (you can add or remove without issues)
- [ ] Minimal understanding of the file, which makes this small, simple, and robust. You may need special handling of the detail sentence however, which is fine.
- [ ] Changes to column values should be picked up as a column change, not addition and removal. GitHub already does that for us!

title: eSIM user provisioning on comma 3X
author: adeebshihadeh
body:
comma 3X ships with an eSIM that comes provisioned from the factory for [comma prime](https://comma.ai/connect). The goal of this bounty is to come up with a solution for users to provision them with their own service, which we can eventually integrate into the device settings or [comma connect](https://connect.comma.ai).

The deliverable is a Python script that sets up an arbitrary eSIM profile on the comma 3X. The input is the eSIM activation code (e.g. `LPA:1$smdp.io$K2-1V5B9M-DD9VC9`).

Notes
* comma 3X uses the [Quectel EG25](https://www.quectel.com/product/lte-eg25-g/)
* comma 3X comes with an [SGP 32](https://www.gsma.com/solutions-and-impact/technologies/esim/gsma_resources/sgp-32-v1-0/) compliant eSIM
* the SIM can also be ordered separately from [here](https://comma.ai/shop/comma-prime-sim) for $20
* may be possible to do this whole bounty on something other than a comma 3X; we just care if it works on a 3X

The EG25 has special firmware with this capability (see [esim.py](https://github.com/commaai/openpilot/blob/master/system/hardware/tici/esim.py)), however the goal of this bounty is to do it without that firmware. 


References:
* https://github.com/estkme-group/lpac
* https://github.com/estkme-group/openeuicc

title: Python bindings for raylib
author: maxime-desroches
body:
## Context:
We eventually want to move all the openpilot UI to raylib instead of Qt. We now have a working version of raylib for both on device and PC (checkout the raylib version of [spinner.cc](https://github.com/commaai/openpilot/blob/master/system/ui/raylib/spinner.cc) and [our fork of raylib](https://github.com/commaai/raylib)). In order to make this transition easy and simple, it would be nice to have python bindings for raylib instead of the current C++ version. raylib has a big [list](https://github.com/raysan5/raylib/blob/master/BINDINGS.md) of python bindings already, but [this one](https://github.com/electronstudio/raylib-python-cffi) seems like the best and most maintained.

## Goal:
Make python bindings for our version of raylib that work on PC and on device. [This one](https://github.com/electronstudio/raylib-python-cffi) seems like the best option. 

**In your PR, include a simple script to build the python wheel.**

To test your wheel, convert `spinner.cc` to `spinner.py` and make sure it works both on PC and on device (raylib built with `RAYLIB_PLATFORM=PLATFORM_COMMA` from [our fork](https://github.com/commaai/raylib)).

title: comma 3X: switch to kernel driver for low level SPI comms
author: adeebshihadeh
body:
The comma 3X talks to its internal panda over SPI using the [spidev](https://www.kernel.org/doc/Documentation/spi/spidev) driver. We want to move the low-level SPI transactions into the kernel for better performance and efficiency.

I already started this a while ago at [panda/drivers/spi/](https://github.com/commaai/panda/tree/master/drivers/spi).

Minimum requirements for this bounty:
* Switch to the kernel driver for the panda Python library (`panda/python/`)
* Make it interrupt driven (no busy waiting!)
* Good auto loading + updating the driver
* Better throughput than the existing panda SPI stack
  * for bonus points, you can patch the kernel to fix the SPI overhead. apparently tracking the "SPI stats" has a ton of overhead in our kernel version, and this was fixed in newer kernels.
* Performance needs to be good enough to support https://github.com/commaai/openpilot/pull/34711
  * since the easiest way to validate may end up being to finish that move, there's an extra $250 on this bounty ($1k total) for finishing that up and merging it together

We'll be switching `pandad` in openpilot to Python soon, so no need to implement this for the C++ panda library (just the one in `panda/python/`).

title: Replace ModemManager with modem.py
author: adeebshihadeh
body:
Let's replace ModemManager with ~200 lines of Python. Faster boot times, better [reliability](https://github.com/commaai/openpilot/blob/b27fa58444766c47f1ef980af79d324a669012c4/system/hardware/hardwared.py#L135), and tinier AGNOS.

---

Bounty targets
* All of openpilot works
* Works on both comma 3X and comma four
* At least 10 Discord users run it for a ~week and sign off on it, both with prime and non-prime service

Misc notes:
* comma 3X uses [Quectel EG25](https://www.quectel.com/product/lte-eg25-g/)
* comma four uses [Quectl EG916](https://www.quectel.com/product/lte-cat-1-bis-eg916q-gl/)
* Let's try not to make the comma 3X and comma four code paths too different if possible
  * comma 3X currently uses QMI, but I think we can get away with just AT commands
* routing priority still needs to work properly (e.g. WiFi > LTE when both are up)
* We'll need to replace dbus, maybe just write a dumb state file to `/dev/shm/modem_state.txt`?

**Bonus target ($500):** `ping -c 1 google.com` works <1 min after powering up a comma four with comma prime.

hunter_intelligence.technical_hint: Focus on implementing AT command handling for both Quectel modems and ensure routing priority logic is preserved while replacing dbus with a lightweight state management approach like a shared memory file.
